title: Accessible Names and Roles
concept: Extracting the ARIA tree to give the agent a structured, semantically rich list of interactive page elements.

exercises.prompt: What is an ARIA tree used for?

options.text: To create a map of a forest.
To extract a list of interactive page elements.

exercises.explain: The ARIA tree helps identify interactive page elements for accessibility.

cards.title: What is an ARIA Tree?
cards.body: The ARIA tree is like a blueprint for a webpage, showing all the interactive elements and their roles.

cards.title: Accessible Names
cards.body: Accessible names are the labels for interactive elements that help users understand their purpose.

cards.title: Like Characters in a Play
cards.body: Each interactive element is like a character in a play, having its own name and role that tells the audience what to expect.

cards.title: Examples of Roles
cards.body: A button might have the role 'button' and an accessible name like 'Submit,' indicating its function.

cards.title: Myth: ARIA trees are only for blind users
cards.body: Wrong! ARIA trees help all users understand and interact with web elements, not just those with disabilities.

cards.title: Visualizing ARIA Trees
cards.body: Imagine a tree with branches representing different elements on a page.

exercises.prompt: What does an accessible name do?

options.text: It tells users the element's purpose.

exercises.explain: An accessible name helps users understand the purpose of that interactive element.

exercises.prompt: True or False: All interactive elements need accessible names.
exercises.explain: True! All interactive elements should have accessible names for clarity.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Accessible names help users understand an element's _______.
exercises.sentence: Accessible names help users understand an element's purpose.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'purpose', as accessible names are meant to clarify the role of the element.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about accessible names is true?

options.text: They are only useful in mobile applications.
They improve usability for everyone, not just those with disabilities.
They can be ignored by screen readers.

exercises.explain: Statement 2 is correct! Accessible names are beneficial for all users, improving overall web usability.

exercises.prompt: Why are ARIA trees important for web accessibility?

options.text: They allow web designers to build faster websites.
They provide a structured list of elements and their roles, making sites more navigable.
They ensure websites look more attractive.
They prevent all usability issues entirely.

exercises.explain: Correct answer 2 highlights that ARIA trees organize elements and roles, enhancing accessibility and navigation.
